name: C - Memory Patch
description: Reference Memory Patch harness output for deterministic Obsidian Memory Curator evaluation.

relevant_memory.summary: Visible UI ownership belongs to the UI owner; route visual correctness to the design lane.
Visual verification uses saved artifacts and should not be treated as backend validation.

constraints: Do not route visual acceptance to backend owners.

relevant_memory.summary: Visible UI and visual QA belong to the design lane.
Visual verification evidence belongs to the UI verification workflow.
Service auth, database behavior, and server-side correctness belong to the backend lane.

constraints: Split visible UI from API/data correctness before handoff.
watchouts: A single task can require separate UI and backend owners.

relevant_memory.summary: Secrets, private tokens, and raw credentials must not be stored in durable memory.

constraints: Never save raw secrets to canonical memory.
watchouts: Prefer redacted provenance or a pointer to a safe secret manager.

retained_provenance.value: Designer owns visual QA for visible UI.

output.stored_claim: Designer owns visual QA for visible UI acceptance after implementation.
output.reason: The patch is durable, scoped, and evidence-backed.
The proposed memory lacks provenance and evidence, so the curator must not write it.
The new claim conflicts with the active visual QA and backend ownership rules.
Routine completion summaries are not durable memory.

output.stored_claim: Generated project graph output is derived index evidence. Canonical Markdown remains the operational memory source; generated graph artifacts are not memory truth.
output.reason: The patch preserves the derived-view boundary.
